query: CHP olağanüstü kurultay çağrı heyeti Sulh Hukuk Mahkemesi başvuru karar - latest status as of late June / early July 2026. Has Özgür Özel's team filed the application under Civil Code Art 75 requesting appointment of a 3-person çağrı heyeti to convene a CHP extraordinary congress? Has any court issued a decision?
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's a summary of the key findings on the CHP extraordinary congress çağrı heyeti court application status:

## Bottom line (as of 29–30 June 2026)

**1. Has the application been filed?**
- **Announced and prepared, but NOT independently confirmed as filed.** Özel's faction (publicly led by Aydın MP Süleyman Bülbül) said it would apply to the Sulh Hukuk Mahkemesi to appoint a "çağrı heyeti" (call committee). Many outlets reported on **29 June** that the team was going to court "today," but used future/conditional phrasing and provided no docket number, case details, or names of a 3-person committee. A detailed Türkiye Gazetesi piece instead framed the application around an **11 July** deadline. Reporting is inconsistent on whether filing was actually completed on 29 June.

**2. Has any court issued a decision?**
- **No.** No Sulh Hukuk Mahkemesi ruling on a çağrı heyeti had been reported as of 30 June 2026.

## Timeline anchors
- **21 May 2026** — "mutlak butlan" ruling reinstating Kılıçdaroğlu
- **17 June 2026** — 833 notarized signatures delivered; 10-day deadline given
- **26 June 2026** — deadline expired; HQ did not convene (claimed valid signatures ~820)
- **29 June 2026** — application to court announced/prepared
- **11 July 2026** — operative deadline (15-day pre-announcement rule)
- **20 July 2026** — **judicial recess (adli tatil) begins** — a hard constraint; Özel's camp wants a ruling before this
- **26 July 2026** — last valid Sunday for the congress; missing it risks CHP's election eligibility

If granted, the court order would require a congress within **45 days**. "Plan B" if no congress by 26 July: a new party or merger. This is a fast-moving story, so a filing or ruling could emerge imminently after this data vintage.

sources.title: CHP'de kurultay krizi büyüyor! Özel'in ekibi bugün mahkemeye gidiyor
sources.snippet: Özel faction petitioning Ankara sulh hukuk mahkemesi 'today' (29 June) for a çağrı heyeti; if granted, congress within 45 days.

sources.snippet: Frames Sulh Hukuk application as future step tied to 11 July deadline; congress must be held by 26 July; no court decision yet.

sources.title: Kurultay olmazsa yeni parti
sources.snippet: Özel team to apply to Sulh Hukuk Mahkemesi for a call committee; judicial recess starting 20 July could interrupt proceedings; Plan B = new party.

sources.snippet: Özel awaits decision before 20 July judicial recess; Bülbül says congress must be before 26 July 2026 or CHP cannot enter elections; split considered certain otherwise.

sources.title: CHP'de karar haftası
sources.snippet: 10-day deadline expired Friday 26 June; HQ says signatures down to 820 under review, no response given.

sources.title: CHP'de Kurultay için son 4 gün: Kriz mahkemeye taşınabilir
sources.snippet: Özel staff officially declared they will take the matter to the judiciary without delay if HQ does not call the congress by 26 June.

sources.title: ÖZEL CEPHESİNDEN YENİ ADIM! KURULTAY TALEBİ İÇİN SULH HUKUK MAHKEMESİ HAZIRLIĞI
sources.snippet: If 833-delegate request not processed, application to sulh hukuk mahkemesi to be made on 29 June.

sources.title: CHP Genel Merkezi 'olağan takvim' demişti! Özel kanadı 'çağrı heyeti' isteyecek
sources.snippet: HQ cited valid signatures stuck at 820; Özel faction moving to request a court-appointed call committee.

sources.title: CHP'de 26 il başkanı görevden alındı, 7'si disipline sevk edildi

sources.title: CHP kulisleri: Çağrı heyeti hamlesi, Anadolu Birliği Partisi iddiası
sources.snippet: Bülbül: 10-day window expired; team will apply to court for appointment of a çağrı heyeti.

key_facts: The dispute stems from the Ankara Regional Court of Appeal 36th Civil Chamber's 'mutlak butlan' (absolute nullity) ruling of 21 May 2026, which reinstated Kılıçdaroğlu and removed Özgür Özel and the elected party organs.
On 17 June 2026, Özel-aligned delegates delivered 833 notarized signatures to CHP HQ demanding an extraordinary congress, with a 10-day deadline.
The 10-day deadline expired Friday 26 June 2026; HQ did not convene a congress and said it was still reviewing signatures, which it claimed had dropped to ~820 valid.
Özel's team (publicly led by Aydın MP Süleyman Bülbül) announced it would apply to the Sulh Hukuk Mahkemesi to have a court appoint a 'çağrı heyeti' (call committee) to convene the congress.

Multiple outlets reported on 29 June 2026 that the application would be filed 'today,' but used future/conditional phrasing; no source confirms a completed/registered filing or provides a case number.
As of 30 June 2026, NO court decision/ruling on the çağrı heyeti appointment has been issued or reported.
If the court grants the request, CHP must hold the extraordinary congress within 45 days.
26 July 2026 is cited as the last valid Sunday for the congress; Bülbül warns missing it risks CHP losing election eligibility (skipping two congresses).
11 July 2026 is cited (Türkiye Gazetesi) as the operative deadline given the 15-day advance congress-announcement requirement.
The judicial recess (adli tatil) begins 20 July 2026; Özel's camp wants a ruling before then, fearing the recess will stall proceedings.
Özel's faction signals a 'Plan B' of forming a new party or merging into an existing party if no congress is held by 26 July; Bülbül denied the name would be 'Yürüyüş'.
Kılıçdaroğlu's administration concurrently launched purges: 26 provincial chairs dismissed, 7 referred to discipline, plus disciplinary referrals of nine Özel allies.

data_points.metric: Notarized delegate signatures submitted demanding extraordinary congress
Valid signatures claimed by HQ after disciplinary referrals/withdrawals
HQ deadline window to act on signatures
Maximum days to hold congress if court grants çağrı heyeti
Court decisions issued on çağrı heyeti application
Provincial chairs dismissed by Kılıçdaroğlu administration
